items.correct: An in-depth qualitative method involving prolonged immersion in a community or setting to understand it from the inside — yielding rich descriptive data about culture, meaning and interaction.
items.misconception: Ethnography is the same as a written survey of a community.
items.random_wrong: Ethnography is the study of different ethnic groups only.
items.why: Ethnography is about method (immersive observation), not topic. Students confuse it with surveys (different method) or limit it to ethnicity (different topic). Ethnographers study workplaces, gangs, hospitals — any setting.

items.q: How did Marx define social class?
items.correct: By relationship to the means of production: the bourgeoisie own productive resources; the proletariat sell their labour. Class is a structural relationship, not just income level.
items.misconception: Marx defined class by how much money people earn.
items.random_wrong: Marx defined class by occupational status like the Registrar General.
items.why: Marx's definition is RELATIONAL (to means of production), not gradational (by income). A highly paid worker is still proletariat; a struggling small business owner can be petite bourgeoisie. Students apply modern income-based thinking to Marx.

items.correct: The unspoken norms, values, and behaviours taught implicitly in schools.
items.misconception: Secret lessons teachers deliberately hide from students.
items.why: Students take 'hidden' too literally as a conspiracy. It refers to things learned implicitly (punctuality, hierarchy, obedience) rather than explicitly taught content.

items.q: Curran & Seaton are linked to which view of the media?
items.why: Correct answer: Marxist. “Pluralist” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: Bandura's Bobo doll experiment is used to support:
items.why: Correct answer: Hypodermic syringe. “Uses and gratifications” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Bandura publish his famous Bobo doll study?
items.why: The real figure is 1961. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
